children.label: i
children.text: the risk to the system from malevolent acts and natural hazards;

children.label: ii
children.text: the resilience of the pipes and constructed conveyances, physical barriers, source water, water collection and intake, pretreatment, treatment, storage and distribution facilities, electronic, computer, or other automated systems (including the security of such systems) which are utilized by the system;

children.label: iii
children.text: the monitoring practices of the system;

children.label: iv
children.text: the financial infrastructure of the system;

children.label: v
children.text: the use, storage, or handling of various chemicals by the system; and

children.label: vi
children.text: the operation and maintenance of the system; and

children.label: B
children.text: may include an evaluation of capital and operational needs for risk and resilience management for the system.

children.label: A
children.text: substantially disrupt the ability of the system to provide a safe and reliable supply of drinking water; or

children.label: B
children.text: otherwise present significant public health or economic concerns to the community served by the system.

children.label: i
children.text: , in the case of systems serving a population of 100,000 or more;March 31, 2020

children.label: ii
children.text: , in the case of systems serving a population of 50,000 or more but less than 100,000; andDecember 31, 2020

children.label: iii
children.text: , in the case of systems serving a population greater than 3,300 but less than 50,000.June 30, 2021

children.heading: Review and revision
children.label: B Review and revision
children.text: Each community water system described in paragraph (1) shall review the assessment of such system conducted under such paragraph at least once every 5 years after the applicable deadline for submission of its certification under subparagraph (A) to determine whether such assessment should be revised. Upon completion of such a review, the community water system shall submit to the Administrator a certification that the system has reviewed its assessment and, if applicable, revised such assessment.

children.label: A
children.text: information that identifies the community water system submitting the certification;

children.label: B
children.text: the date of the certification; and

children.label: C
children.text: a statement that the community water system has conducted, reviewed, or revised the assessment, as applicable.

children.heading: Provision to other entities
children.label: 5 Provision to other entities
children.text: No community water system shall be required under State or local law to provide an assessment described in this section (or revision thereof) to any State, regional, or local governmental entity solely by reason of the requirement set forth in paragraph (3) that the system submit a certification to the Administrator.
strategies and resources to improve the resilience of the system, including the physical security and cybersecurity of the system;

plans and procedures that can be implemented, and identification of equipment that can be utilized, in the event of a malevolent act or natural hazard that threatens the ability of the community water system to deliver safe drinking water;

actions, procedures, and equipment which can obviate or significantly lessen the impact of a malevolent act or natural hazard on the public health and the safety and supply of drinking water provided to communities and individuals, including the development of alternative source water options, relocation of water intakes, and construction of flood protection barriers; and
strategies that can be used to aid in the detection of malevolent acts or natural hazards that threaten the security or resilience of the system.

children.heading: Coordination
children.label: c Coordination
children.text: Community water systems shall, to the extent possible, coordinate with existing local emergency planning committees established pursuant to the Emergency Planning and Community Right-To-Know Act of 1986 ( et seq.) when preparing or revising an assessment or emergency response plan under this section.42 U.S.C. 11001

children.heading: Record maintenance
children.label: d Record maintenance
children.text: Each community water system shall maintain a copy of the assessment conducted under subsection (a) and the emergency response plan prepared under subsection (b) (including any revised assessment or plan) for 5 years after the date on which a certification of such assessment or plan is submitted to the Administrator under this section.

children.heading: Guidance to small public water systems
children.label: e Guidance to small public water systems
children.text: The Administrator shall provide guidance and technical assistance to community water systems serving a population of less than 3,300 persons on how to conduct resilience assessments, prepare emergency response plans, and address threats from malevolent acts and natural hazards that threaten to disrupt the provision of safe drinking water or significantly affect the public health or significantly affect the safety or supply of drinking water provided to communities and individuals.

children.heading: Alternative preparedness and operational resilience programs
children.label: f Alternative preparedness and operational resilience programs
A
children.text: using and complying with technical standards that the Administrator has recognized under paragraph (2); and

children.label: B
children.text: submitting to the Administrator a certification that the community water system is complying with subparagraph (A).

children.heading: Authority to recognize
children.label: 2 Authority to recognize
children.text: Consistent with section 12(d) of the National Technology Transfer and Advancement Act of 1995, the Administrator shall recognize technical standards that are developed or adopted by third-party organizations or voluntary consensus standards bodies that carry out the objectives or activities required by this section as a means of satisfying the requirements under subsection (a) or (b).

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Administrator shall establish and implement a program, to be known as the Drinking Water Infrastructure Risk and Resilience Program, under which the Administrator may award grants in each of fiscal years 2020 and 2021 to owners or operators of community water systems for the purpose of increasing the resilience of such community water systems.

children.label: A
children.text: the purchase and installation of equipment for detection of drinking water contaminants or malevolent acts;

children.label: B
children.text: the purchase and installation of fencing, gating, lighting, or security cameras;

children.label: C
children.text: the tamper-proofing of manhole covers, fire hydrants, and valve boxes;

children.label: D
children.text: the purchase and installation of improved treatment technologies and equipment to improve the resilience of the system;

children.label: E
children.text: improvements to electronic, computer, financial, or other automated systems and remote systems;

children.label: F
children.text: participation in training programs, and the purchase of training manuals and guidance materials, relating to security and resilience;

children.label: G
children.text: improvements in the use, storage, or handling of chemicals by the community water system;

children.label: H
children.text: security screening of employees or contractor support services;

children.label: I
children.text: equipment necessary to support emergency power or water supply, including standby and mobile sources; and

children.label: J
children.text: the development of alternative source water options, relocation of water intakes, and construction of flood protection barriers.

children.heading: Exclusions
children.label: 3 Exclusions
children.text: A grant under this subsection may not be used for personnel costs, or for monitoring, operation, or maintenance of facilities, equipment, or systems.

children.heading: Technical assistance
children.label: 4 Technical assistance
children.text: For each fiscal year, the Administrator may use not more than $5,000,000 from the funds made available to carry out this subsection to provide technical assistance to community water systems to assist in responding to and alleviating a vulnerability that would substantially disrupt the ability of the system to provide a safe and reliable supply of drinking water (including sources of water for such systems) which the Administrator determines to present an immediate and urgent need.

children.heading: Grants for small systems
children.label: 5 Grants for small systems
children.text: For each fiscal year, the Administrator may use not more than $10,000,000 from the funds made available to carry out this subsection to make grants to community water systems serving a population of less than 3,300 persons, or nonprofit organizations receiving assistance under , for activities and projects undertaken in accordance with the guidance provided to such systems under subsection (e) of this section.section 300j–1(e) of this title

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: 6 Authorization of appropriations
children.text: To carry out this subsection, there are authorized to be appropriated $25,000,000 for each of fiscal years 2020 and 2021.

the term “resilience” means the ability of a community water system or an asset of a community water system to adapt to or withstand the effects of a malevolent act or natural hazard without interruption to the asset’s or system’s function, or if the function is interrupted, to rapidly return to a normal operating condition; and
the term “natural hazard” means a natural event that threatens the functioning of a community water system, including an earthquake, tornado, flood, hurricane, wildfire, and hydrologic changes.
